---
title: "Housing Dashboard (Plotly)"
description: "Interactive dashboard for exploring housing prices across major U.S. cities."
format: html
execute:
  enabled: true
---

## 📊 Housing Price by City

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go

df = pd.read_csv("../../data/ZHVI.csv")

df["date"] = pd.to_datetime(df["date"])

cities = ["New York, NY", "Los Angeles, CA", "San Diego, CA", "Dallas, TX"]

filtered = df[df["RegionName"].isin(cities)]

fig = go.Figure()

for i, city in enumerate(cities):
    city_df = filtered[filtered["RegionName"] == city]

    fig.add_trace(
        go.Scatter(
            x=city_df["date"],
            y=city_df["value"],
            mode="lines",
            name=city,
            visible=(i == 0)
        )
    )


buttons = []

for i, city in enumerate(cities):
    visible = [False] * len(cities)
    visible[i] = True

    buttons.append(
        dict(
            label=city,
            method="update",
            args=[
                {"visible": visible},
                {"title": f"Housing Prices in {city}"}
            ]
        )
    )


fig.update_layout(
    updatemenus=[
        dict(
            buttons=buttons,
            direction="down",
            showactive=True,
        )
    ],
    title=f"Housing Prices in {cities[0]}",
    xaxis_title="Date",
    yaxis_title="Price",
    template="plotly_white"
)

fig
```
